name: Lanikai
desc: Postcard-perfect beach enclave — million-dollar Mokulua Island views, exclusive, no commercial anything, one road in/out.
insider: Park at the public beach access paths on Mokulua Drive — there are 11 numbered paths between houses leading to the sand
Kayak to the Mokes (Mokulua Islands) early morning before the trade winds pick up — pristine beaches with almost nobody there
Parking enforcement is aggressive after 8am — residential permit zones are strictly enforced so park in Kailua and walk or bike
The Lanikai Pillbox hike (Kaiwa Ridge Trail) takes 20 minutes and has the best sunrise view in all of windward Oahu
There are zero commercial businesses in Lanikai — no stores, no restaurants, no public restrooms at the beach
Median home prices are $3M+ and tiny lots sell for over $2M just for the land — this is some of the priciest real estate in Hawaii
The Mokulua Islands are state bird sanctuaries — you can land on Moku Nui but Moku Iki is off-limits to protect nesting seabirds
The beach is narrow and loses sand seasonally — winter months can reduce the beach to almost nothing in some sections
Lanikai was originally a vacation cottage community in the 1920s — some original cottages still exist among the mansions
The one road in and out (Aalapapa Drive / Mokulua Drive loop) creates horrible traffic — avoid the loop during rush hours
